messages.message:
Compilation Skipped: Existing memoization could not be preserved

React Compiler has skipped optimizing this component because the existing manual memoization could not be preserved. The inferred dependencies did not match the manually specified dependencies, which could cause the value to change more or less frequently than expected. The inferred dependency was `profile`, but the source dependencies were [profile?.id]. Inferred less specific property than source.

  388 |
  389 |   // ÔöÇÔöÇ Fetch notifications ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ
> 390 |   const fetchNotifications = useCallback(async () => {
      |                                          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 391 |     if (!profile?.id) return;
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 392 |     const { data } = await supabase
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 393 |       .from("notifications_with_actor")
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 394 |       .select("*")
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 395 |       .eq("user_id", profile.id)
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 396 |       .order("created_at", { ascending: false })
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 397 |       .limit(30);
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 398 |     setNotifications(data || []);
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 399 |   }, [profile?.id]);
      | ^^^^ Could not preserve existing manual memoization
  400 |
  401 |   // ÔöÇÔöÇ Realtime subscription ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ
  402 |   useEffect(() => {

Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

  402 |   useEffect(() => {
  403 |     if (!profile?.id) return;
> 404 |     fetchNotifications();
      |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  405 |
  406 |     const channel = supabase
  407 |       .channel(`notifs-${profile.id}`)
React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'isOpen'. Either include it or remove the dependency array.

messages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

  454 |   // ÔöÇÔöÇ Fermer dropdown sur changement de route ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ

Error: Cannot access refs during render

React refs are values that are not needed for rendering. Refs should only be accessed outside of render, such as in event handlers or effects. Accessing a ref value (the `current` property) during render can cause your component not to update as expected (https://react.dev/reference/react/useRef).

  534 |
  535 |   return (
> 536 |     <div ref={ctx.bellRef}>
      |               ^^^^^^^^^^^ Cannot access ref value during render
  537 |       <NotificationBell
  538 |         unreadCount={ctx.unreadCount}
  539 |         notifications={ctx.notifications}
Fast refresh only works when a file only exports components. Use a new file to share constants or functions between components.

* Update external systems with the latest state from React.

messages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

  101 |
  102 |   useEffect(() => {
> 103 |     fetch();
      |     ^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  104 |     const ch = supabase
  105 |       .channel("lb-rt")
  106 |       .on("postgres_changes", { event: "UPDATE", schema: "public", table: "player_stats" }, fetch)

messages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

  102 |   useEffect(() => {
  103 |     if (!user) {
> 104 |       setPermissions({
      |       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  105 |         can: () => false,
  106 |         isSuperAdmin: false,
  107 |         isAdmin: false,
